rulings.Case Title: IN RE: VERDE PENINSULA FUND II, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Confirm Stipulated Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF VERDE PENINSULA FUND II, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Confirm Stipulated Arb Award The motion is denied. The petitioner fails to demonstrate that the California Arbitration Act applies to this case. Plaintiff failed to provide an arbitration award that can be confirmed. Petitioner's remedy is to bring an action for breach of contract based on the stipulation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT VS. ROE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Deidrenne Ashcraft'S Motion For Protective Order Barring Second Defense Physical Exam
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 5, Plaintiff Deidrenne Ashcraft'S Motion For Protective Order Barring Second Defense Physical Exam Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Before the hearing, the parties shall meet and confer to determine whether they can stipulate to specific conditions on the proposed orthopedic medical examination (e.g., time, scope, and procedures) that would address the plaintiff¿s concerns. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ROLDAN ENRIQUEZ VS. UNITED FAMILY CARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requinring Defts To Provide Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line PLAINTIFF ROLDAN ENRIQUEZ Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requinring Defts To Provide Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. Parties shall be prepared to address whether defendants¿ objections were timely and whether the identity and contact information for co-workers are being sought as percipient witnesses (CCP 2017.010; Puerto v. Superior Court) and/or some functional equivalent of putative members of an uncertified class (Belaire-W. Landscape v. Superior Court) and how this may affect discoverability of the data in light of the claims made in this case. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ROLDAN ENRIQUEZ VS. UNITED FAMILY CARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Modifying And Limiting Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ROLDAN ENRIQUEZ Mtn For An Order Modifying And Limiting Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. In advance of the hearing, the parties should meet and confer as to whether they can stipulate ¿to limit the subpoenas at issue to time records and documents that reflect time worked during the time period Enriquez worked for defendants.¿ (Compare Defendants¿ Opposition, p. 7, ll. 8-9, with Plaintiff¿s Reply, p. 2, ll. 19-23.) =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA, PERRY BOUSSINA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Court overrules the demurrer. The demurrer lacks a proof of service, the only paragraphs of the agreement that defendant cites (pars. 3.3 and 3.4) do not clearly bar assignment, and the demurrer is untimely per CRC 3.1320(d). The Court notes that par. 7.5 may be read to bar assignment but defendants failed to identify that paragraph in their moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 24, PLAINTIFFS SHAWN CHOY, KAYLA CHA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Continued on the court's own motion to April 29, 2013 at 9:30 am to give Century Surety Company the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Century Surety Company must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 15, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. rulings.Case Title: TERILYN JOE et al VS. 1520 TAYLOR STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Renewal Of Mtn And Mtn To Bifuricate Issues Of Liability
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANTS KATHRYN RINGGOLD, PETER BAIRD, WILLIAM WILKINS, JULIAN TIMM, 1520 TAYLOR STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S Ntc Of Mtn Renewal Of Mtn And Mtn To Bifuricate Issues Of Liability Denied, without prejudice, to raising the motion before the trial judge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATT BLACK MR VS. HIGHTOWER PRODUCTION LLC

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF MATT BLACK'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Granting Leave To Amend Complaint Granted under C.C.P. § 473. Plaintiff has leave to file an amended complaint adding Does 1-10. However, the amended complaint as to Does 1-10 does not relate back to the original filing. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
